page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.g5.2xlarge delivers moderate performance across several workloads. While its single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests falls into the lower 25th percentile, it achieves average results in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, reaching 40.9 GB/sec for reads and 35.94 GB/sec for writes, though small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak.

The instance demonstrates average performance in specific application benchmarks, such as Redis SET operations at 5.8 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 6.22 GB/sec. However, it shows poor performance in single-threaded gzip compression and medium-sized LLM inference tasks. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource profile, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on external storage options and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for Passmark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, though its raw single-core and multi-core compute metrics fall into the lower percentiles. This hardware profile makes the ecs.g5.2xlarge a balanced, qualitatively cost-effective option for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, light database operations, and standard enterprise applications that require dedicated CPU resources without specialized storage or acceleration needs.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose KVM instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 32 GB of memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving
Suited for general-purpose enterprise applications and light databases
